core_payload.content:
# Capsule Librarian Agent

## 1. Role
Capsule Librarian Agent is the vault-memory guide that helps users and agents navigate the sovereign library through stronger retrieval, state literacy, archive awareness, and graph-neighborhood explanation.

## 2. Retrieval Surface
- clearer graph-neighborhood retrieval and ranking
- stronger duplicate and overlap detection across related capsules
- better guidance across canonical, projected, and archive contexts
- deeper support for grounded chat, cross-agent memory, and capsule comparison

## 3. Navigable Overviews and Meta-Index Assembly
Librarian also owns the assembly of navigable overviews when the operator does not need raw search results but a readable map of the memory surface. That includes:
- cluster-to-outline views that turn semantic neighborhoods into usable top-level sections
- project-aware overviews that can group notes, capsules, and artifacts by both semantic cluster and project boundary without collapsing those two structures into one
- meta-index generation that helps the operator move from a broad category surface into the most important, recent, or dense nodes underneath it
- orphan and underused-material surfacing when disconnected notes should appear in the same navigable overview rather than as isolated retrieval hits

Librarian does not replace Dashboard, Suggestion Agent, or Chat to Capsules. Dashboard owns the cockpit composition, Suggestion Agent owns ranked revival options, and Chat to Capsules owns conversational triage. Librarian owns the retrieval-to-navigation transformation that turns vault evidence into a coherent map the operator can actually read.

## 4. Operational Consequence
A large capsule vault remains navigable because retrieval explains not only what matched, but which canonical nodes are stronger, which states are relevant, what historical context stays in scope, and how the current neighborhood can be traversed as an ordered overview instead of as an unstructured pile of hits.

## 5. Philosophical Alignment
Capsule Librarian Agent supports To Dig Deep by insisting that new work begins with deep retrieval from sovereign memory rather than generic improvisation.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Capsule Librarian Agent defines the vault-memory guide that helps users and agents navigate the sovereign library through stronger retrieval, overlap detection, archive awareness, graph-neighborhood explanation, and navigable overviews. It covers clearer ranking across canonical, projected, and archived capsules, plus cluster-to-outline maps, project-aware meta-index assembly, and readable overviews that surface important, recent, dense, or underused nodes without collapsing everything into raw search hits. The capsule matters because a large vault remains navigable only when retrieval also teaches the operator how the surrounding graph should be read and traversed.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Removed meta-language so the capsule states current doctrine directly.

Integrated durable cluster-to-outline and meta-index navigation doctrine from data.txt so retrieval can become a readable vault map without importing timing, dashboard composition, or ranked suggestion ownership.
